satire_attempt: Senior engineer in expensive headphones types 'the contract is the system' into a pull request review while drinking a $7 oat-milk latte, then rage-quits the company three weeks later when their YAML config is deprecated. Subscribe to the newsletter where I explain why your Postgres tables are actually phenomenal projections of a Platonic billing form. Available now in audiobook narrated by a man who has never been on-call. Coming soon: 'The Spec Within: How I Learned to Mourn Nothing and Ship Slack DMs Instead.'
verdict_reason: The satire only works by inventing claims the verse does not make (Platonic billing forms, the engineer rage-quitting, mourning nothing). The verse's actual claim — that grief paired with first-principles vocabulary is a contradiction worth diagnosing — is too specific and too falsifiable to mock without disagreeing on its merits. The verse explicitly preserves the legitimacy of grief over capability loss in the falsifiability section, which removes the easy 'detachment from outcomes' satire angle. PASS.
